acceptance_targets: [chapter_12_craft_001] From `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:199` through the exit beat at `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215`, there is no more than one paragraph of static reflection before a concrete physical escalation occurs, and Mina's route out is visibly harder by the time she reaches daylight.

[chapter_12_prose_001] In the revised narration at `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:47`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:107`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:177`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223`, and `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231`, every standalone narrative sentence has an explicit subject and finite verb unless it is quoted in-world text.

findings.acceptance_test: From `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:199` through the exit beat at `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215`, there is no more than one paragraph of static reflection before a concrete physical escalation occurs, and Mina's route out is visibly harder by the time she reaches daylight.
findings.evidence: After the tide turn is introduced, the exit pauses for poster-reading, a darkness test, and one more reflective tank beat before the water materially tightens the route (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:199`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:203`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:205`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:211`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215`).
findings.problem: The chapter reintroduces external danger, then suspends it for several nonessential interpretive beats. That lets the exit leak urgency just after the badge revelation should have forced the scene into a tighter physical squeeze.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the exit sequence anchored at `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:199`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:203`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:205`, and `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:211` so that once Mina hears the tide returning, each paragraph either escalates the flooding/space problem or delivers a final indispensable discovery. Compress or remove the poster/darkness pause if it is only reiterating meaning the chapter has already landed.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised narration at `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:47`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:107`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:177`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223`, and `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231`, every standalone narrative sentence has an explicit subject and finite verb unless it is quoted in-world text.
findings.evidence: The narration repeatedly uses clipped fragments rather than complete sentences, including "Pumps, trickles, fans..." (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:47`), "Then the sluice." (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:107`), "Forgot the mud..." (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:177`), "Easy to miss by water." (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223`), and "Maybe not innocent..." (`snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231`).
findings.problem: This chapter violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipped narration often enough that the prose starts reading as mannered terseness rather than controlled compression. The issue is not brevity; it is repeated omission of subjects or finite verbs in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: At `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:47`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:107`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:177`, `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223`, and `snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231`, rewrite the narration into grammatically complete sentences or fold the fragments into adjacent clauses while keeping Mina's close-third snap. Preserve clipped document language only where it is quoted logbook, signage, or other in-world text rather than narration.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's uncomfortable overlaps: desire entangled with access, wonder contaminated by biotech history, class need shaping erotic choices, and ecological harm rendered at creature scale. When a scene sits on the edge between restraint and retreat, keep the pressure visible through bodies, residue, changed behavior, or social cost. If an exact detail would make the material more embarrassing, more sensual, or more morally compromising, that is usually the detail to keep.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, ecological damage, class shame, or moral compromise are softened into tasteful fog at the moment they should bite, the scene has failed and must be rewritten at higher specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms should signal deliberate emphasis, institutional performance, or a character-specific formality shift.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compression and dilation aggressively: single-scene nocturnes, comic paperwork chapters, quiet survey chapters, domestic weather chapters, confrontational set pieces, and aftermath chapters should sit beside each other without repeating the same rhythm two chapters in a row.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, coastal, and exact without brochure prettiness; scientific and bureaucratic terms may appear when the characters would think or speak through them, but they should stay embodied by consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Mina Cordero, with free-indirect access tight enough to track bodily desire, shame, and observational obsession without collapsing into confessional summary
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple sentence music that can hold humidity, rot, and erotic charge, then cut hard into blunt compression at moments of humiliation, procedural friction, or ecological damage.
